activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I think the outdoor concert will attract a large audience.
activities.explanation: La oración expresa una opinión sobre el futuro introducida por «I think», por lo que «will» es adecuado.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Perhaps our neighbours will join us for dinner.
activities.explanation: «Perhaps» presenta una posibilidad futura sin indicios visibles; «will» comunica correctamente la predicción.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I'm sure Maya will enjoy the photography course.
activities.explanation: «I'm sure» expresa la valoración del hablante; la construcción correcta es «will + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: In my opinion, remote work will remain common.
activities.explanation: La marca «In my opinion» deja claro que se trata de una predicción basada en una opinión.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I expect the repair will take about two hours.
activities.explanation: «I expect» introduce una expectativa del hablante y admite naturalmente «will».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Do you think the team will reach the final?
activities.explanation: La pregunta solicita una opinión sobre un resultado futuro; «will + infinitivo» es la forma esperada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Scientists believe the new method will reduce water use.
activities.explanation: «Believe» presenta una predicción razonada, pero no una consecuencia de evidencia inmediata visible.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: The forecast says temperatures will fall overnight.
activities.explanation: Se informa de una predicción meteorológica; «will fall» es natural y gramatical.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I don't suppose the shop will be open after nine.
activities.explanation: «I don't suppose» expresa una expectativa negativa; la negación recae en «suppose» y «will» mantiene la predicción.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: One day, this neighbourhood will look completely different.
activities.explanation: «One day» sitúa una predicción general en un futuro no concretado; «will» encaja con esa perspectiva.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I think she will passes the driving test.
activities.explanation: Después de «will» debe aparecer el infinitivo sin terminación: «will pass», no «will passes».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Perhaps the parcel will to arrive tomorrow.
activities.explanation: «Will» va seguido del infinitivo sin «to»: la forma correcta es «will arrive».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I doubt the meeting won't finish before six.
activities.explanation: Con «I doubt» no se añade normalmente otra negación: «I doubt the meeting will finish before six».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I imagine the museum won't be very crowded on Monday morning.
activities.explanation: «I imagine» introduce una predicción personal negativa; «won't be» está bien construido.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Will people still use cash in twenty years' time?
activities.explanation: La pregunta pide una predicción general sobre el futuro y usa correctamente «Will + sujeto + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I don't think the battery will last all day.
activities.explanation: En inglés es natural colocar la negación en «I don't think» y mantener «will last» en la subordinada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: Most experts predict that the population will continue to grow.
activities.explanation: El verbo «predict» presenta explícitamente una predicción; «will continue» es correcto.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I am certain the changes will not affect existing customers.
activities.explanation: «I am certain» marca una valoración segura del hablante y «will not affect» expresa la predicción negativa.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: There will probably be fewer petrol cars on city streets by 2040.
activities.explanation: En la construcción existencial, «probably» se coloca naturalmente después de «will»: «There will probably be».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: The course probably won't require any previous experience.
activities.explanation: «Probably» puede preceder a «won't» en una predicción negativa; la estructura posterior usa el infinitivo «require».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I wonder whether the new library will have study rooms.
activities.explanation: Tras «I wonder whether» se mantiene el orden afirmativo: «the library will have», no inversión de pregunta.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I wonder whether will the new library have study rooms.
activities.explanation: Es una pregunta indirecta y no admite inversión: debe decir «whether the new library will have».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: It is unlikely that food prices will fall this winter.
activities.explanation: «It is unlikely that» valora la probabilidad de un hecho futuro y se combina correctamente con «will fall».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I expect that, once the bridge opens, journey times will become shorter.
activities.explanation: La predicción principal usa «will»; tras el conector temporal «once» aparece presente simple, no futuro.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence uses the future form appropriately.
activities.prompt: I expect that, once the bridge will open, journey times will become shorter.
activities.explanation: En una oración temporal futura con «once» usamos presente simple: «once the bridge opens».
